article_title: Qualcomm Launches First Wi-Fi 8 Chip at MWC 2026 as 40-Company Coalition Commits to 6G by 2029

content_preview.title: Qualcomm Launches First Wi-Fi 8 Chip at MWC 2026 as 40-Company Coalition Commits to 6G by 2029
content_preview.summary: Qualcomm's FastConnect 8800 doubles Wi-Fi speeds to 11.6 Gbps while a 40-company coalition including Ericsson, Google, and Microsoft formally targets 6G commercialization starting 2029.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Mobile World Congress 2026, running March 2–5 in Barcelona, opened with two significant wireless connectivity announcements. Qualcomm introduced the FastConnect 8800, its first chip supporting Wi-Fi 8, claiming double the speeds and triple the range of its previous generation. Separately, a coalition of over 40 industry leaders — including Ericsson, Google, Microsoft, Samsung, Nokia, and Amazon — formally committed to launching commercial 6G networks beginning in 2029, backed by liv...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured, technically detailed article covering two distinct MWC 2026 announcements — Qualcomm's FastConnect 8800 Wi-Fi 8 chip and the 6G coalition formalization. The piece uses clear section headings, appropriate technical depth for a News piece, and a solid 'What We Don't Know' section that adds analytical value without overclaiming.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 sources verified as reachable and reputable tech publications. Non-allowlist sources (Android Authority, NotebookCheck, TechRadar, TelecomTV, The Fast Mode) are established industry outlets. Round 2 corrections verified independently: (1) Apple is confirmed absent from the Qualcomm-led 6G coalition in both TelecomTV and the original BusinessWire release; (2) Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite stats (40% throughput, 2.5x latency, 30% energy) are confirmed real Qualcomm figures corroborated across NewsBytesApp and Android Central, and TechRadar's coverage of the MWC 2026 Dragonwing platform announcement provides a reasonable citation; (3) TelecomTV article at telecomtv.com/content/6g/qualcomm-and-ericsson-move-6g-from-concept-to-proof-setting-a-clear-path-to-commercialization-54951/ confirmed to explicitly state '400 MHz component carrier with 30 kHz subcarrier spacing' and '4 transmit receive antennas', making the citation correction valid.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All three findings from Round 1 have been correctly addressed. Apple has been removed from the coalition paragraph in both body and summary. The BGR citation for Dragonwing NPro A8 Elite specs has been replaced with TechRadar, which covers the same Dragonwing Wi-Fi 8 announcement at MWC 2026. The OTA demo technical parameters (30 kHz subcarrier spacing, 4 antennas) are now correctly attributed to the first TelecomTV article, which explicitly contains these specifications. No new factual issues identified.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing or sensationalism. The analysis section makes reasonable inferences about industry trends (AI connectivity demands, 6G as programmable infrastructure) without overreaching.
editor_notes.originality: No duplicate coverage in the published article archive. No existing article on MWC 2026, Wi-Fi 8, FastConnect 8800, or the 6G coalition. Topic is novel and timely.
recommendations: The non-allowlist sources (Android Authority, NotebookCheck, TechRadar, TelecomTV, The Fast Mode) are all reputable industry publications frequently used in tech journalism. Consider adding them to config/source_allowlist.txt for future submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Round 2 submission successfully addresses all three issues identified in Round 1. The article is factually accurate, well-sourced, neutral in tone, and meets all editorial standards. Approved for publication.
